cards.title: Hydrotherapy — Overview & Types
cards._legacyHtml:
Definition: Therapeutic use of water in any form (liquid, steam, ice) at varying temperatures and pressures.
Key treatments:
Sitz Bath (Hip Bath): Patient sits in tub of water covering hips/lower abdomen. Cold (50–65°F): constipation, haemorrhoids, atonic pelvic organs. Hot (104–110°F): pelvic pain, dysmenorrhoea, prostatitis. Alternate/Contrast: chronic pelvic congestion, IBS.
Spinal Bath: Long tub, water at lumbar level. Cold (65–85°F): nervous system toning, hypertension, insomnia, mental disorders. 20–30 min.
Immersion Bath (Full Bath): Hot (104–110°F): detox, muscle relaxation, flu. Neutral (97–99°F): insomnia, anxiety, psychosis. Cold (50–65°F): fever, stimulating tonic.
Foot Bath: Hot foot bath: headache (reflex action), cold extremities, nasal congestion. Contrast foot bath (3 min hot + 1 min cold × 5): peripheral circulation, diabetic neuropathy (mild only — check sensation first).

cards.title: Hydrotherapy — Advanced Treatments
cards._legacyHtml:
Wet Sheet Pack: Patient wrapped in cold wet sheet, then blankets. Stages: Tonic (pack stays cold — stimulating), Neutral (warms to body temp — sedating), Heating (hot and sweating — eliminative). Duration: 20–60 min. Indications: fever management, detox, insomnia, skin diseases, toxaemia.
Fomentation (Hot Compress): Wrung-out hot towel applied to area. 2–3 min hot, brief cold, repeat × 3. Indications: muscle spasm, joint pain, pleuritis, abdominal pain.
Compress (Cold): Cold wet towel kept on area and changed as it warms. Fever (forehead), headache, acute injuries (RICE), inflammation (acute phase), conjunctivitis.
Steam Bath: Whole-body steam 108–115°F, 10–15 min. Eliminates toxins via skin (sweating). Respiratory conditions (steam inhalation). Follow immediately with cold shower.
Jacuzzi / Whirlpool: Warm water + hydrodynamic jets. Hydrotherapy for musculoskeletal conditions, post-surgical rehabilitation, sports injuries, chronic pain.

cards.title: Mud Therapy — Types & Applications
cards._legacyHtml:
Ideal mud: Black cotton soil or Fuller's earth (Multani mitti). Collected from 3–4 feet depth. Sun-dried, sieved. Store in earthen pot. Never use contaminated or chemically-exposed soil.
Abdominal Mud Pack: Navel to pubic symphysis. Cold mud. 20–30 min, morning before breakfast. Indications: constipation, IBS, indigestion, hyperacidity. Most commonly prescribed mud application in BNYS.
Eye Mud Pack: Cool mud pads over closed eyes. 20 min. Indications: eye strain, conjunctivitis, headache, sinusitis. Removes dark circles.
Head Mud Pack: Cool mud applied to scalp. 30 min. Indications: headache, hair fall, dandruff, hypertension.
Mud Bath (Full): Whole body mud application. 45–60 min in sun. Indications: skin diseases (psoriasis, eczema, vitiligo), arthritis, general debility. Follow with cold water bath.
Local Mud Pack: Applied to any joint or lesion. Cold for acute inflammation, warm for chronic stiffness.
Precautions: Avoid over open wounds, very sensitive skin. Do not expose to cold wind after mud bath.

cards.title: Massage Therapy — Techniques
cards._legacyHtml:
Basic techniques:
Effleurage (Stroking): Long, smooth strokes towards heart. Begins and ends every massage. Prepares tissues, aids lymphatic and venous return. Pressure: light to firm.
Petrissage (Kneading): Grasping and squeezing muscle. Releases tension, improves circulation. Used on large muscle groups (trapezius, gluteals, calf).
Friction: Deep circular pressure on specific points. Breaks adhesions, treats chronic muscle knots, scar tissue. Can be painful — communicate with patient.
Tapotement (Percussion): Hacking, cupping, tapping. Stimulating. Used on back and large muscles. Contraindicated over bony prominences and kidney area.
Vibration: Rapid oscillation. Stimulates nerves, loosens mucus (chest physiotherapy). Fine vibration: relaxing. Coarse vibration: stimulating.
Indian (Abhyanga): Oil massage with warm sesame/Ayurvedic oil. Head to toe, directional strokes. Benefits: nourishes skin, ↓ Vata, promotes sleep, enhances circulation.
Massage duration: Full body: 45–60 min. Localised: 15–20 min.

cards.title: Therapeutic Fasting — Types
cards._legacyHtml:
Water Fasting: Only water (2–3 L/day). Most intense form. Duration: 1–7 days under supervision. Indications: acute toxaemia, severe obesity, pre-surgery detox. Requires medical supervision for >3 days.
Juice Fasting: Fresh vegetable/fruit juices only. 500 ml–1.5 L/day. 3–14 days. More sustainable than water fast. Indications: chronic disease management, weight loss, liver detox. Most common clinical prescription in BNYS.
Fruit Monofast: One fruit only (grapes, apples, watermelon) for 3–7 days. Grape cure (Brandt): historically used in naturopathy for cancer detox. Gentle, well-tolerated.
Intermittent Fasting (16:8): Eat within 8-hour window, fast 16 hours daily. Most evidence-based form. Indications: insulin resistance, obesity, metabolic syndrome. Sustainable long-term.
5:2 Fast: Normal eating 5 days, very low calorie (500 kcal) 2 non-consecutive days. Comparable results to daily restriction. Good for adherence.
Alkaline/Raw Fast: Only raw fruits and vegetables. Gentle. 7–21 days. Indications: skin diseases, chronic constipation, detox.

cards.title: Acupressure & Marma — Key Points
cards._legacyHtml:
Technique: Apply firm, steady pressure with thumb or knuckle. 30–60 seconds per point. 3–5 times per session. Can be slightly uncomfortable but not painful.
Key therapeutic points:
Headache/Migraine: LI4 (Hegu — webbing between thumb and index finger), GB20 (at base of skull, both sides), Yintang (between eyebrows).
Nausea/Vomiting: PC6 (Neiguan — 3 finger-widths above wrist, inner arm). Evidence Level 1A for chemotherapy nausea, morning sickness, post-op nausea.
Lower Back Pain: BL23 (1.5 cun lateral to L2 spinous process), BL40 (centre of popliteal crease), GV4 (between L2–L3).
Insomnia: HT7 (Shen Men — wrist crease, ulnar side), SP6 (3 cun above medial malleolus), K1 (centre of sole).
Constipation: ST36 (Zusanli — 4 finger-widths below knee, lateral tibial tuberosity), ST25 (2 cun lateral to navel), LI4.
Marma point Nabhi (Navel): Clockwise massage around navel — strengthens Agni, improves digestion. 5 min daily with warm sesame oil.

cards.title: Chromotherapy — Quick Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
Quick clinical guide:
Red: Stimulating. Anaemia, hypothyroidism, paralysis, depression, low BP. Avoid: fever, hypertension.
Orange: Warming, anti-spasmodic. Asthma, amenorrhoea, constipation, lactation. Avoid: anxiety, fever.
Yellow: Nerve tonic, digestive. Constipation, liver, skin, diabetes, mental dullness. Avoid: diarrhoea.
Green: Balancing, antiseptic. Hypertension, infection, headache, ulcers. Universal balancer — safest colour.
Blue: Cooling, sedating. Fever, inflammation, insomnia, burns, hypertension, tachycardia. Avoid: depression.
Indigo: Pain-reducing, parathyroid. Hyperthyroidism, cataracts, acute pain, convulsions. Avoid: hypothyroidism.
Violet: Sedative, lymphatic. Epilepsy, insomnia, neurological, lymphadenopathy. Avoid: weakness, anaemia.
Solarised water dose: 30–60 ml, 3x/day before meals. Prepare: 4–8 hours direct sunlight through coloured glass bottle (3/4 filled).

cards.title: Magneto Therapy — Quick Reference
cards._legacyHtml:
North pole (N) — Sedating, anti-inflammatory: Applies for: acute pain, infection, fever, inflammation, insomnia, hypertension, headache.
South pole (S) — Stimulating: Applies for: paralysis, chronic fatigue, hypothyroidism, poor circulation, delayed wound healing (post-acute).
Magnetic water: North pole water (alkaline) — anti-inflammatory: 100 ml 3x/day. South pole water (acidic) — digestive: 50–100 ml before meals.
Session: 15–30 min. Static magnet directly on site.
Condition guide:
• Arthritis (acute): N pole
• Arthritis (chronic): S pole
• Fracture healing: S pole (after acute phase)
• Insomnia: N pole on forehead/temples
• Constipation: S pole on abdomen
• Hypertension: N pole on cardiac area
• Sinusitis: N pole on sinus area
Absolute contraindications: Pacemaker, cochlear implants, pregnancy (pelvic area), active bleeding, metallic implants at site.
